title: Build the Root
description: Compare all candidate splits and choose the root of the tree.
takeaways: The root split compares every feature and threshold candidate.
The winning root split has the lowest weighted Gini impurity.
A pure child branch can become a leaf immediately.

practiceLab.objective: Evaluate Age and resting_bp midpoint thresholds, track impurity reduction, and choose the root split.
instructions: Run the dataset cell and inspect the six-row patient table.
Run the helpers cell and note the parent Gini plus candidate thresholds.
Run the guided loop that scores every (feature, threshold) pair.
Inspect the candidates DataFrame sorted by weighted_gini.
Complete Exercise 1 by selecting root_split as the top (lowest weighted Gini) row.
Complete Exercise 2 by partitioning the data with root_split and printing each child's labels and Gini.
Confirm impurity reduction is parent Gini minus weighted Gini for the winner.
Answer the reflection questions about minimize-vs-maximize and impure children.
hints: Candidate thresholds are midpoints between sorted unique feature values.
Sort candidates by weighted_gini ascending; the first row is the root.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: A ranked candidates table, a printed root_split row, and left/right label lists with their Gini scores.
